stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced 22 Apr 2026: net profit up 16% YoY to Rs 1,353.8 cr, revenue up 12.6% YoY to Rs 15,076 cr. FY26 EBIT up 39.2% YoY to Rs 7,152 cr; margins expanded for 10th consecutive quarter. Final dividend of Rs 36/share (total FY26 dividend Rs 51 - highest ever); record date 3 Jul 2026. Headcount fell by ~2,000 in Q4. Stock around Rs 1,477.90 with one-month gain of ~7.7%.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT is one of the worst-performing sectoral indices in 2026, down ~16.9% YTD (cited as 30-month low). Sustained FII selling in Feb-Mar 2026 amid AI/tariff concerns. Q4 results season underway with TECHM, Infosys reporting; FY27 guidance is the key sentiment driver.
stocks.peer_comparison: TECHM weight in Nifty IT is 5.71%; TCS dominates at 36.55%. Among large-cap IT, TECHM's Q4 print and margin trajectory is relatively strong. PE ~27.6, P/B ~4.6. During Feb sell-off, TECHM dropped >6% in single session alongside LTIM, Persistent, Coforge.
red_flags: Headcount reduction of ~2,000 in Q4 FY26 signals demand caution
IT sector under sustained FII selling pressure (AI, US tariff fears)
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 36/share, record date 3 Jul 2026

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 303.85 as of 27 Apr 2026, market cap Rs 3.81 lakh cr. Acquired majority Board nomination rights in Sproutlife effective 1 Apr 2026 (foods portfolio expansion). Interim dividend of Rs 6.50/share paid 27 Feb 2026. Q3 FY26 net profit fell 6% YoY on labour costs but revenue beat estimates. Q4 FY26 results pending - will be the first full quarter under the new tobacco tax regime.
stocks.sector_context: Major regulatory shock: GST on tobacco hiked from 28% to 40% effective 1 Feb 2026; central excise on cigarettes raised to Rs 2,050-8,500 per 1,000 sticks; NCCD set to rise further in May 2026. ITC has passed through 19-41% price hikes on Gold Flake, Classic. Broader FMCG sector expects high single-digit volume growth, but tobacco volumes are shrinking.
stocks.peer_comparison: ITC PE ~18 vs Nestle >70, HUL ~45; significantly cheaper than FMCG peers, but discount reflects cigarette tax overhang. Non-cig FMCG segment Rs 6,800 cr in Q4 FY26, growing 12% YoY. Dividend yield 4.72% - high relative to peers.
red_flags: GST hike to 40% on tobacco effective Feb 2026 - first full-impact quarter is Q4
ITC stock had dropped ~25% on tax shock (~$7B m-cap loss); brokerages including Motilal Oswal downgraded
NCCD hike scheduled for May 2026 adds further pressure
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results pending - key data point for tax pass-through impact
Final dividend announcement with Q4 results

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 301.60 on 28 Apr 2026, hit a 52-week high of Rs 298.6 (+4.44%) on the back of crude rally - up ~5% over prior two sessions. Board appointed Yogish Nayak S. as CFO effective 1 May 2026; approved Rs 25 cr JVC stake and IGGL support. Minor fire at SHP Platform, Mumbai High on 3 Apr 2026 (10 minor injuries, contained quickly). Second interim dividend of Rs 6.25/share paid (record 18 Feb 2026). Q3 FY26 net profit jumped 16.67% YoY to Rs 10,015.78 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Brent crude rose to $111.16/bbl on 28 Apr 2026 (+2.71% d/d) - last seen in March; driven by stalled US-Iran ceasefire efforts and Strait of Hormuz risk. Strong tailwind for upstream (ONGC, Oil India); negative for OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL).
stocks.peer_comparison: Upstream peers ONGC and Oil India both rallied on crude; OMCs declined on cost pressure. ONGC at 52-week high outperforming the broader market on geopolitical premium.
red_flags: Recent fire incident at SHP Platform (Mumbai High) - operational risk reminder
CFO transition pending 1 May 2026 - leadership change just before Q4 results
Crude rally is geopolitically driven; reversal on US-Iran ceasefire would compress upside
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results pending - benefit from elevated crude realizations
New CFO Yogish Nayak takes charge 1 May 2026

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced 24 Apr 2026: returned to profit at Rs 594 cr vs loss of Rs 2,329 cr YoY (and Rs 128 cr in Q3). FY26 net profit Rs 889 cr vs Rs 2,576 cr in FY25 (down ~65%). Final dividend Rs 1.50/share (15% rate). Appointed two independent directors; designated Ganesh Sankaran and Jagdeep Mallareddy as executive directors. Stock at ~Rs 780-848 in late April 2026; market cap Rs 60,700 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Bank index weighted toward HDFC Bank (24.96%) and ICICI Bank (19.63%); INDUSINDBK only 1.36%. Large lenders FD rates 6.45-6.50%. Private banking sector overall stable; INDUSINDBK is the clear underperformer on idiosyncratic governance issues.
stocks.peer_comparison: 1-year return: ICICI +24.09%, HDFC +23.03%, INDUSINDBK -47.40% (worst in pack). ICICI net NPA 0.37%, HDFC 0.42%, vs INDUSINDBK gross NPA 3.43% (deteriorated from 3.13% YoY). Trading at ~10x trailing PE, deep discount to peers reflecting risk.
red_flags: SFIO probe (initiated Dec 2025) into Rs 2,601.94 cr derivatives/accounting irregularities (FY16-FY24)
SEBI insider trading bans on senior executives (Kathpalia sold 125k shares avoiding Rs 5.2 cr loss; Khurana sold 340k+ shares avoiding Rs 14.3 cr loss before disclosure)
Former CEO Sumant Kathpalia and Deputy CEO Arun Khurana resigned over the issue
FY26 PAT down 65% YoY; gross NPA worsened YoY to 3.43%
Big-4 audit firms summoned by SFIO
catalysts: Q4 turnaround to profit may lift sentiment short-term
Final dividend Rs 1.50/share record date pending
New leadership team (executive directors) signals stabilization attempt

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced 27 Apr 2026: net profit up 11.15% YoY to Rs 10,849 cr; consolidated revenue up 5.75% YoY to Rs 46,490 cr. Final dividend Rs 5.25/share (total FY26 dividend Rs 26.75). Stock surged 4.45-4.72% on 28 Apr 2026 to intraday high of Rs 473.90, closing around Rs 467. CMPDI (subsidiary) listed on BSE/NSE 30 Mar 2026 after IPO at Rs 172/share (1.071 cr shares OFS). FY26 standalone PAT Rs 18,863.93 cr vs Rs 17,061.56 cr in FY25.
stocks.sector_context: PSU mining/coal sector benefits from steady power demand. Coal India is the world's largest coal miner with dominant market share, robust FCF, ROCE 35-48%, dividend yield 5.8-6.9% - top among PSU dividend payers. Centre planning major coal mining reforms benefiting Coal India, NTPC, etc.
stocks.peer_comparison: NTPC (power) at Rs 411.40 on 27 Apr; classified as strong performer with high financial strength. Coal India's dividend yield ~6% materially higher than NTPC's 2-3.5%. MOFSL sees 12% upside on Coal India and projects 4% volume CAGR over FY26-28E with revenue/EBITDA CAGRs of 5%/12%.
red_flags: Diesel price cost pressure flagged ahead
Dependency on government policy and e-auction premiums for margin growth
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 5.25/share record date pending AGM approval
Q4 results positive momentum; stock at near 52-week levels
